payment_terms.rationale: Aligns with our AP cycle and standard buyer-side practice. Net-60 strains working capital and is unusual for SaaS subscriptions in our size range.
payment_terms.fallback: Net-45 acceptable if vendor offers a meaningful discount or if the subscription is critical-path

late_fees.position: 1% per month maximum if any late fee is included
late_fees.rationale: 1.5% is above market for buyer-side terms. We prefer no late fee but will accept a token rate.

liability_cap.position: 1x fees paid in the 12 months preceding the claim
liability_cap.rationale: Mutual cap at 1x fees is our standard floor for SaaS. Caps tied to 'fees paid' (vs. fees payable) reduce vendor exposure beyond what we view as proportionate.
carve_outs_required: Data breach involving personal data (supercap of 3x or insurance coverage limits)

indemnification.position: Mutual indemnity for IP infringement and confidentiality breach
indemnification.rationale: We expect symmetry on indemnities of equal weight. Vendor IP indemnity should be matched by customer indemnity for IP claims arising from our data and any customizations we provide.

auto_renewal.position: No auto-renewal preferred; if accepted, 30-day cancellation notice maximum
auto_renewal.rationale: Evergreen with long notice windows traps customers in unwanted renewals. Our procurement cycle typically catches 30-day notices; 60+ days creates calendar risk.

subcontracting.position: Notice required + customer approval for material subcontractors (defined as those processing customer data or providing >25% of services)
subcontracting.rationale: We need visibility into the data-handling supply chain for compliance and security reviews. Vendor flowdown of obligations is required.

termination_for_breach.position: Symmetric cure periods (30/30 or 60/60); customer should not face longer cure than vendor
termination_for_breach.rationale: Asymmetric cure periods favor vendor and limit customer remedies. We require parity.

dispute_resolution.position: Arbitration acceptable in customer's home state OR mutually agreed neutral location
dispute_resolution.rationale: Vendor-home-state arbitration imposes travel cost on us in any dispute. We prefer neutral or customer-state forum.

data_security.position: SOC 2 Type II minimum, breach notification within 24-72 hours, annual penetration test reports available, customer audit rights for material incidents
data_security.rationale: Standard floor for any SaaS vendor handling our operational data.

insurance.position: Cyber/E&O minimum $5M, GL $2M, certificate of insurance on request, customer named as additional insured for E&O
insurance.rationale: Coverage levels must match the data-handling risk profile.

ip.position: Vendor retains platform IP (acceptable). Customer owns Customer Data (acceptable). Customer outputs and derived analytics generated for Customer should be Customer-owned or jointly owned.
ip.rationale: Vendor platform IP retention is standard. The treatment of customer-specific outputs and analytics is often glossed over and we want it clear.

confidentiality.position: Mutual; minimum 5-year tail for non-trade-secret confidential information
confidentiality.rationale: 3-year tails are short relative to the lifecycle of competitive information. 5 years is our floor.

deal_breakers: Vendor unilateral audit rights against customer
Broad customer indemnity (e.g., for any third-party claim arising from use of the service)
Evergreen renewal with notice period > 90 days
negotiation_style: We negotiate but pick our battles. We push hard on liability cap, indemnity symmetry, and data-security floors. We are flexible on payment terms and renewal mechanics if the deal is otherwise strong.
